title: 10 best herbal essential oils for nasal congestion
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for nasal congestion are natural remedies that help alleviate sinus pressure and congestion by opening airways and reducing inflammation. These oils, particularly Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Mentha x piperita, Lavandula angustifolia, Cinnamomum verum, Hyptis suaveolens, and Ocimum basilicum, have decongestant properties that ease breathing. If left untreated, nasal congestion can lead to headaches, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life, sleep, and work performance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains a compound called eucalyptol, which is known to break down mucus and relieve sinus pressure. When inhaled, the oil's vapors help to open up airways, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. This allows for easier breathing and can provide rapid relief from symptoms of colds, allergies, and other respiratory conditions. Regular use of Tasmanian blue gum essential oils may also help to soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: A natural decongestant and expectorant, eucalyptol helps to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion.
Cineole: A potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory, cineole helps to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
Methyleugenol: A natural bronchodilator, methyleugenol helps to open up airways and relieve respiratory congestion by expanding the lungs and improving breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make essential oil as they have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and relieve congestion in the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks are rich in flavonoids, which can help to thin mucus and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from sustainable sources.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for at least 7 days to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the dried leaves using 10 grams of leaves per 100 ml of water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel into a dark glass bottle.
Store the finished eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and alpha-pinene, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, tea tree oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat underlying infections and sinusitis, further alleviating congestion. As a result, using tea tree essential oils in a humidifier or through inhalation can provide rapid relief from stuffy noses and promote a sense of clear-headedness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the nasal passages, reducing congestion and promoting drainage.
Decongestant: The eucalyptol content in tea tree oil has natural decongestant properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant: Tea tree oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cineole, a compound that helps to ease nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Buds: Contain camphor, which has natural decongestant properties that help relieve stuffy noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at dawn or dusk for maximum oil yield and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Place the dried leaves in a clean glass jar with a lid and steam distill them using a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles.
Store the finished melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, work to open up airways and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, lemongrass has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat infection and swelling, further alleviating congestion. When inhaled, the oil's vapors also help loosen mucus and promote healthy sinus drainage, providing fast and effective relief from stuffy noses.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass essential oil helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.
Decongestant properties: The camphene present in lemongrass essential oil has a natural ability to clear mucus from the nasal passages, helping to relieve congestion and promote easy breathing.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass essential oil contains compounds that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their ability to effectively clear respiratory congestion and ease breathing.
Rhizomes: The rhizome of lemongrass is rich in citral, a compound responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it an effective remedy for nasal congestion and sinusitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a sterile cloth and air circulation for 24 hours.
Steam-distill the dried leaves using a water bath at 100°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oils.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue by centrifugation and decantation within 30 minutes of collection.
Store the extracted cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, rosemary essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, further alleviating congestion. When inhaled, it can also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel. Overall, rosemary essential oils is a natural and effective remedy for relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Rosemary essential oil has natural antihistamine effects, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve nasal congestion caused by allergies.
Decongestant properties: The eucalyptol content in rosemary oil acts as a natural decongestant, helping to clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, providing instant relief from stuffiness.
Antibacterial properties: Rosemary essential oil has antibacterial properties that help combat infections caused by bacteria, such as sinusitis, which can contribute to chronic nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and relieving sinus pressure.
Leaves: Packed with camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which help to thin mucus, ease congestion, and open up airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning when dew is present for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air dry them naturally.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a stainless steel still and water at a ratio of 1:4 for 30 minutes to extract oil.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight lid and keep it refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to effectively combat inflammation and reduce excess mucus production in the nasal passages. When inhaled, these compounds help to open up airways, making it easier to breathe and promoting a sense of relief from congestion. Additionally, thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties may also help to combat underlying infections that can contribute to nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Thyme essential oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
Antimicrobial: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that can cause or exacerbate nasal congestion, such as colds and sinusitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help to decongest and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Thyme leaves have a high concentration of menthol and camphor, which provide instant relief from nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn or early morning when their oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves using a still and water at a ratio of 1:4.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation mixture after 2-3 hours of steam distillation.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil relaxes the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages. This allows for easier breathing and clearer airways, making it an effective remedy for relieving stuffy noses and sinus pressure. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out mucus, further alleviating congestion and promoting a sense of relief from respiratory discomfort.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has natural decongestant properties, which help to open up airways and relieve sinus pressure and congestion.
Menthol: Menthol's cooling and warming effects help to thin mucus and ease its removal from the body, reducing nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Camphor: Camphor has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and menthone, peppermint roots are excellent for creating essential oils that ease congestion and relieve respiratory issues.
Leaves: The fragrant leaves of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which makes them ideal for producing essential oils that effectively decongest the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from the garden or store-bought dried peppermint herb.
Combine one cup of dried peppermint herb with two cups of water in a heat-resistant bowl.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in boiling water, allowing the flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the infused liquid to cool and then transfer it to an essential oil distiller or small pot, simmering for 30-45 minutes to produce the essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from stuffiness. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can also help to relax the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate congestion.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: English Lavender essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain viruses and bacteria that can cause nasal congestion, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Decongestant properties: The oil's natural decongestant compounds help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from stuffiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender essential oil has been found to have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots are often used to create essential oils that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Buds: Fresh lavender buds are prized for their high concentration of camphor, a natural decongestant that helps open up airways and relieve sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves in the early morning or evening when aromatic oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves using a dehydrator at 35°c for 12 hours or air-drying method for several weeks.
Combine 200g of dried leaves with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains a compound called terpinen-4-ol, which has been shown to help reduce inflammation and open up airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, juniper's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the nose and sinuses, further alleviating congestion. When inhaled, the oil can help loosen mucus and reduce sinus pressure, providing quick relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Juniper essential oil has natural decongestant properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and sinus pressure.
Expectorant: The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal cavity, making it easier to breathe and promoting a faster recovery.
Anti-inflammatory: Juniper essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinusitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion by soothing inflamed tissues.
Buds: The buds of the juniper plant contain volatile oils that provide decongestant properties, helping to relieve stuffy noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh juniperus communis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation.
Combine 20 grams of chopped juniper material with 100 milliliters of water in a stainless steel pot.
Steam distill the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to extract essential oil.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a glass separation funnel and cotton wool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thujone and camphor, work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, hyssop's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it an effective remedy for congestion caused by colds, allergies, or sinus infections. When inhaled, the oil helps to loosen mucus, promoting a clearer airway and relief from stuffiness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Hyssop essential oil helps to relax and calm the muscles in the nose and sinuses, relieving constriction and reducing nasal congestion.
Expectorant property: Hyssop oil helps to loosen and expel mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Hyssop essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are a rich source of essential oils that can help relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and decongest nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves in the morning when they are most aromatic and potent with scissors cutting at the base of stems for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out evenly on paper towels or clean cloth for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steam-distill the dried leaves using a still equipped with a condenser coil and collection flask filled with distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for three hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a dark glass vial by carefully pouring it off the surface of the water into a separate container with a pipette, then seal with a tight-fitting cap.
Store the freshly extracted hyssopus officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight and heat for at least one week to allow the oil to mature and settle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that target the nasal passages. The oil contains a compound called calendulic acid, which reduces swelling and constriction of blood vessels in the nose, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, pot marigold oil's natural antiseptic and decongestant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, providing fast relief from sinus pressure and congestion. As a result, using pot marigold essential oils can provide rapid and effective alleviation of nasal congestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages, helping to relieve sinus pressure and discomfort.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of Calendula essential oil can help combat infections that cause nasal congestion by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can irritate the nasal membranes.
Expectorant: Calendula essential oil has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of pot marigold contain sesquiterpenes, which help to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Buds: Pot marigold buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease congestion and soothe irritated nasal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated at dawn.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried calendula officinalis flowers using 10 grams of botanical material per 100 milliliters of distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the condensate by decanting or filtering through a coffee filter.
Store the calendula officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nasal congestion with herbal essential oils is to inhale a blend of peppermint, eucalyptus, and lavender oils. Add 5-7 drops of each oil to a diffuser or inhale them directly from a cloth or handkerchief. You can also mix them with carrier oils like coconut or olive oil for a nasal congestion-relieving rub. Inhaling these oils opens airways, reduces inflammation, and promotes decongestion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nasal congestion that are treated with herbal essential oils are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and sinusitis. Certain oils like eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. Other ailments, including allergies, colds, and coughs, can also be alleviated with the use of herbal essential oils in aromatherapy or topical applications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its decongestant properties, which relieve sinus pressure and inflammation. The oil's active compound, eucalyptol, has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. When inhaled, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils can reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relieving congestion caused by colds, allergies, or sinus infections. Its invigorating aroma also helps to open airways and improve respiratory function.
remedy_constituents: 1,8-Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps thin mucus and relieve congestion by increasing the secretion of mucus and promoting its expulsion from the respiratory tract.
α-Pinene: This terpene acts as a bronchodilator, which means it helps to relax the muscles in the airways and improve airflow, reducing nasal congestion.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain a high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant.
Buds: The buds are used due to their high eucalyptol content and are often used as a more potent alternative to leaves.
Barks: The barks are also used to produce essential oils for nasal congestion, although in smaller quantities, as they contain a lower concentration of eucalyptol compared to leaves and buds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 kg of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator funnel.
Filter the essential oil using a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's primary component, camphor, acts as a bronchodilator, relaxing airway muscles and allowing mucus to drain more easily. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby relieving nasal congestion. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the nasal passages, further contributing to its decongestant effects and providing relief from stuffy noses. This makes it a popular natural remedy for sinus and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: This terpene helps to reduce nasal congestion by acting as an expectorant, loosening and clearing mucus from the nasal passages.
Camphor: This terpene has a decongestant effect by increasing blood flow to the nasal area and thinning mucus, making it easier to breathe.
Bornene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain high amounts of camphor and borneol, which help decongest the nasal passages.
Stems: They are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain compounds like camphor and verbenone, which have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: They are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain high amounts of camphor, borneol, and other compounds that help relieve nasal congestion and promote sinus drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves on a dry sunny morning between june and september.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours to produce the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it using paper filters.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.
Label and date the bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its potent decongestant properties. The oil contains compounds such as thymol and carvacrol, which have natural mucolytic and expectorant properties. These components help break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and promoting easier breathing. Additionally, Thymus vulgaris essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, further alleviating congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Carvacrol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses.
Bornyl acetate: Possesses mucolytic properties, breaking down mucus and making it easier to expel, thereby relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain high concentrations of thymol and carvacrol, which have decongestant and antibacterial properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they have a higher content of thymol and other volatile compounds that help to relieve congestion.

Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils and have a more potent decongestant effect than leaves or flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at dawn when they are at their peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove impurities and improve its quality.
Store the final thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with nasal congestion because its active compound, cineole, has natural decongestant properties. When inhaled, it helps to thin mucus, making it easier to breathe and clear out nasal passages. This allows for improved airflow, reducing sinus pressure and congestion. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections that contribute to nasal congestion. By providing relief from congestion, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can promote a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This monoterpene helps to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Limonene: This terpene has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thus alleviating nasal congestion.
α-Terpineol: This terpene exhibits bronchodilatory effects, which help to relax the muscles in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are primarily used due to their high concentration of cineole, a key compound responsible for the oil's decongestant properties.
Buds: The buds are used for their high cineole content, which helps to reduce nasal congestion and promote ease of breathing.
Leaves/Stems: Leaves and stems are combined in some cases, as they both contain cineole and other beneficial compounds that contribute to the oil's decongestant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves of the melaleuca alternifolia plant when they are fresh and in high quantity for efficient processing.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly by removing dirt and debris to ensure purity and quality of the final product.
Subject the cleaned leaves to steam distillation to extract the essential oil from the plant material.
Collect the extracted essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separator.
Store the extracted essential oil in airtight containers to preserve its quality and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its decongestant properties, which are attributed to the presence of citral and limonene compounds. These natural chemicals work to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. The invigorating aroma of lemongrass essential oil also helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation, providing relief from stuffiness and congestion. As a natural expectorant, Cymbopogon citratus essential oil aids in the removal of excess mucus, promoting a sense of ease and relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Geranial: This citral isomer helps with nasal congestion by its decongestant and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving congestion and promoting the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract.
Linalool: This terpene helps with nasal congestion by its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which reduce swelling and open up airways, making breathing easier.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide helps with nasal congestion by its anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil from Cymbopogon citratus leaves is used to alleviate nasal congestion due to its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The essential oil from Cymbopogon citratus rhyzomes is used to relieve nasal congestion due to its decongestant, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties.
Stems: The essential oil from Cymbopogon citratus stems is used to ease nasal congestion due to its decongestant, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature of 15-25°c.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place to preserve the oil's quality and potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with nasal congestion because it has natural decongestant properties that help to clear out mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The oil's menthol content acts as a bronchodilator, opening up airways and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and cough up excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and promoting a clearer respiratory tract. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Menthone acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion.
Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the nasal mucosa, which helps to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.
Limonene: Limonene has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are often used to make essential oils for nasal congestion due to their high menthol content, which helps to open airways and relieve congestion.
Stems: Mentha x piperita stems are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion because they contain a higher concentration of menthone, a natural decongestant.

Buds: Mentha x piperita buds are used to make essential oils for nasal congestion as they have a higher concentration of menthol and menthone, which helps to ease congestion and promote respiratory relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from your garden in the morning when they are at their peak oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves and flowers in a stainless steel distillation apparatus at 100-150mmhg for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and discard the aqueous phase that contains impurities and water.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and debris from the distillation process.
Store the filtered mentha x piperita essential oil in dark glass bottles away from light and heat to preserve its aroma and properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties and ability to thin mucus. The oil's high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate work together to open airways, reduce inflammation, and promote drainage. As a result, it eases congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the nasal passages, providing relief from sinus pressure and discomfort associated with colds, allergies, and sinus infections.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, helping to open up airways and reduce congestion.
Beta-caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, thereby relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most used part of Lavandula angustifolia for essential oils due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have decongestant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are often used due to their ability to retain the plant's fragrance and contain similar compounds to the flowers, providing a similar effect for nasal congestion.

Stems: The stems are sometimes used for essential oil production, as they contain a smaller amount of the same decongestant compounds found in the flowers and leaves, making them a suitable alternative.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried flowers at a temperature of 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the condenser and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like eugenol and cinnamaldehyde, which have natural anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and open up airways. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help fight off infections that can cause congestion. By promoting healthy mucus production and facilitating the removal of excess mucus, Cinnamomum verum essential oils can provide relief from nasal congestion and promote easier breathing.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound helps with nasal congestion by acting as a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Cinnamyl acetate: This terpene helps to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the body, thereby relieving nasal congestion and sinus pressure.

Linalool: This terpene exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages, thereby relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil extracted from Cinnamomum verum leaves is used for its decongestant properties to help relieve nasal congestion.
Buds: The buds of Cinnamomum verum contain oils that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Stems: The essential oil extracted from Cinnamomum verum stems is used to help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the cinnamomum verum bark by cutting off the inner bark from mature trees.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at room temperature for 2 to 3 weeks.
Grind the dried bark into small pieces using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 cup of the ground cinnamomum verum with 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains compounds like bornyl acetate, linalool, and camphor, which work together to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages. Its expectorant properties help to loosen and remove phlegm, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, further alleviating congestion and promoting a more open airway. This natural remedy offers relief from nasal congestion without harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Helps to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby reducing congestion.
Eugenol: Exhibits expectorant properties, facilitating the expulsion of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract.
β-Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in volatile compounds, particularly camphor and pinene, which help in decongesting and clearing the nasal passages.
Flowers: The flowers contain oils with expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Stems: The stems of Hyptis suaveolens contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which help in relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh stems of hyptis suaveolens at dawn when the dew is still present for maximum oil retention.
Steam distill the harvested stems in a still at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the essential oils by condensing the steam and separating the oil from the water through settling.
Filter the collected essential oils through a cotton cloth or filter paper to remove impurities and particles.
Store the filtered essential oils in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dry place for long-term preservation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum essential oils helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's expectorant action helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, promoting easy breathing. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and redness in the nasal membranes, further alleviating congestion. Additionally, the oil's ability to warm and soothe the nasal passages makes it a popular natural remedy for relieving nasal congestion and promoting overall respiratory health. Its fragrance also provides a calming effect.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It helps to reduce nasal congestion by exhibiting bronchodilatory properties, which relaxes the airway muscles and eases breathing.
Eugenol: It reduces nasal congestion by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, which decreases swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses.
Limonene: It helps to alleviate nasal congestion by its expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content and therapeutic properties that help ease respiratory issues.
Stems: The stems are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce congestion and soothe the nasal passages.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their decongestant and expectorant properties, which help relieve nasal congestion and promote drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves on a warm sunny day when the plants are in full bloom.
Steam distill the ocimum basilicum leaves in a distillation apparatus for 2 hours at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled water and essential oil mixture in a separate container for 30 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel for 10 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities for 5 minutes.
